verses.text: Woe to those who decree unjust laws to rob the poor, widows, and orphans; 'what will ye do in the day of visitation?' Oppressive lawmaking is cursed. When God comes to reckon, the unjust will have nowhere to flee.

Woe to those who decree unjust laws to rob the poor, widows, and orphans; 'what will ye do in the day of visitation?' Oppressive lawmaking is cursed. When God comes to reckon, the unjust will have nowhere to flee.

Assyria is 'the rod of mine anger,' sent against a godless nation; yet the proud king means only to destroy, not knowing he serves God's purpose. The tyrant is God's tool. He rages for his own ends, but God wields him for His own.

Assyria is 'the rod of mine anger,' sent against a godless nation; yet the proud king means only to destroy, not knowing he serves God's purpose. The tyrant is God's tool. He rages for his own ends, but God wields him for His own.

Assyria is 'the rod of mine anger,' sent against a godless nation; yet the proud king means only to destroy, not knowing he serves God's purpose. The tyrant is God's tool. He rages for his own ends, but God wields him for His own.

The Assyrian boasts that his princes are kings and his conquests prove no god can resist him; he will treat Jerusalem like Samaria. Pride knows no limit. He measures the living God by the idols he has overthrown.

The Assyrian boasts that his princes are kings and his conquests prove no god can resist him; he will treat Jerusalem like Samaria. Pride knows no limit. He measures the living God by the idols he has overthrown.

The Assyrian boasts that his princes are kings and his conquests prove no god can resist him; he will treat Jerusalem like Samaria. Pride knows no limit. He measures the living God by the idols he has overthrown.

When the Lord finishes His work on Zion, He will punish 'the stout heart of the king of Assyria,' who boasted his own hand did it all. Pride is the next to fall. The instrument that exalts itself will itself be broken.

When the Lord finishes His work on Zion, He will punish 'the stout heart of the king of Assyria,' who boasted his own hand did it all. Pride is the next to fall. The instrument that exalts itself will itself be broken.

When the Lord finishes His work on Zion, He will punish 'the stout heart of the king of Assyria,' who boasted his own hand did it all. Pride is the next to fall. The instrument that exalts itself will itself be broken.

Shall the axe boast against him that hews with it? So God will send leanness among Assyria's fat ones and kindle a fire. The tool is not greater than its wielder. The proud forget they are nothing without the hand that uses them.

Shall the axe boast against him that hews with it? So God will send leanness among Assyria's fat ones and kindle a fire. The tool is not greater than its wielder. The proud forget they are nothing without the hand that uses them.

'The light of Israel shall be for a fire,' burning the Assyrian's thorns in a day, till the remnant of his trees are so few a child could count them. His vast host is consumed. God's holiness is a flame that devours the proud.

In that day the remnant of Israel shall no more lean on their conqueror but 'stay upon the LORD... in truth.' Affliction teaches true reliance. The surviving few learn to rest on God alone.

'O my people... be not afraid of the Assyrian'; his yoke shall be broken from off your neck. God comforts the fearful. The oppressor's burden will be lifted when God's anger turns from His people to their foe.

'O my people... be not afraid of the Assyrian'; his yoke shall be broken from off your neck. God comforts the fearful. The oppressor's burden will be lifted when God's anger turns from His people to their foe.

The Assyrian advances town by town toward Jerusalem, shaking his fist at Zion in confident menace. Ruin seems imminent. The enemy comes near, but his triumph is not allowed to be complete.

Then the Lord lops the boughs with terror and hews down the high ones; the proud forest of Assyria falls. God topples them at the height of their threat. When the wicked seem strongest, He brings them suddenly low.
